verdictContract: ACCEPT iff signature authentic AND recovered signer == vendor AND a full-directory rebuild binds byte-for-byte to the sealed payload; otherwise REJECT.
note: Language-agnostic conformance vectors. Any implementation of the evidence-seal verifier (Go, Rust, ...) MUST reproduce expectedVerdict/expectedExit for every case. Fields prefixed with `_` are informational (frozen ground-truth digests), not verifier inputs.

cases.name: genuine-single
cases.localized: all files re-derive; root matches; signer==vendor
cases.note: Baseline: one genuine file, correct issuer pinned. The strongest ACCEPT.

cases.name: genuine-multi
cases.localized: 4 files incl. nested (docs/, data/) re-derive; root matches; signer==vendor
cases.note: Multi-file + nested-directory genuine set; exercises the sorted-leaf Merkle root over a tree.

cases.name: tampered-file
cases.note: One sealed file's bytes were altered after sealing. evidence verify localizes it as CHANGED; the canonical signed gate fails the content bind.

cases.name: wrong-vendor
cases.localizedAs: signature genuine but recovers to a DIFFERENT issuer than pinned
cases.note: Bytes + packet are 100% genuine, but the consumer pins a DIFFERENT expected issuer. Content passes; the issuer PIN fails. This is the 'right file, wrong signer' reject.

cases.name: missing-file
cases.note: A file named in the seal is absent on disk. evidence verify localizes it as MISSING.

cases.name: extra-file
cases.localizedAs: UNEXPECTED-injected.txt present on disk but not named in the seal
cases.note: An extra file the seal never committed sits alongside the genuine set. The canonical signed gate REBINDS against a FULL directory scan, so the injected file breaks the bind (REJECT). NOTE: `vh evidence verify` alone re-derives only NAMED files and would ACCEPT — a conformant full verifier MUST bind against the whole directory.
